Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Hi Hongchao,

"The removal of the "stop file" is also improved not to
trigger the test error if it has been deleted.

Could you please explain how "stop file" can be removed? IMO, it could be removed only in the end of test in stack_trap handler. It looks more like the stop file hasn't been created causing ENOENT in the end of the test.

Failed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest from trevis-108vm13: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/404b69fc-9299-4b07-96e3-2b4a98824e67 ran 5 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> (3 comments)
> 
> Hi Hongchao,
> 
> "The removal of the "stop file" is also improved not to
> trigger the test error if it has been deleted.
> 
> Could you please explain how "stop file" can be removed? IMO, it could be removed only in the end of test in stack_trap handler. It looks more like the stop file hasn't been created causing ENOENT in the end of the test.

Yes, as per the test script, the stop file could only be deleted by the stack_trap.
but the test logs shew there is no error in the call ' do_facet mds1 "touch $stopf" ' just like the error shown during ' do_facet mds1 "rm -f $stopf" '.

Could it be the file not created even if the "touch" command is running without problem?
will add an extra check for it.

Patch Set 3:

Is this actually a fix, or just a new way to debug/detect the failure?

Clearly, creation of the "stop file" shouldn't ever fail (maybe unless /tmp is full, or out of inodes if that can even happen separately), so I think the test would still fail as often as before, but just in a different way.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Is this actually a fix, or just a new way to debug/detect the failure?
> 
> Clearly, creation of the "stop file" shouldn't ever fail (maybe unless /tmp is full, or out of inodes if that can even happen separately), so I think the test would still fail as often as before, but just in a different way.

You are right - it is a way to debug the failure. We don't understand why it fails at this moment.

Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.subject: LU-15461 test: add stop file check
commit.message:
LU-15461 test: add stop file check

Adding the creation checking of "stop file".

Change-Id: I4acd36e61faf4259c2821293ffb7913d4cca76bd
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-quota
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao@whamcloud.com>
